@graph.description:
We want to recreate the evaluation in Cramer et al. 2022 PNAS, the COVID-19 Forecast Hub probabilistic-forecast paper. The dataset is the public Reich Lab Forecast Hub on GitHub (reichlab/covid19-forecast-hub), with the Johns Hopkins CSSE COVID-19 dashboard repository (CSSEGISandData/COVID-19) as the ground truth. The evaluation period is epidemiological week 17 of 2020 through EW42 of 2021, 79 weeks total. The targets are 1-, 2-, 3-, and 4-week-ahead incident deaths. Submissions cover 57 locations (50 states, DC, and 6 territories: American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, Puerto Rico, US Virgin Islands), of which 55 are evaluated after excluding American Samoa and Northern Mariana Islands (zero deaths in window), plus a national forecast. Each model submits 23 probability quantiles per target per location per week.

The eligibility criteria for inclusion are: each model is designated primary by its team, covers at least 25 of 51 focal locations, includes all four forecast horizons, includes the full 23-quantile set, and submitted in at least 47 of 79 weeks. After applying these criteria, 28 models qualify. We want to reproduce Table 1 of the paper -- per-model relative WIS, relative MAE, 50% PI coverage, and 95% PI coverage.

The headline finding to verify is that the COVIDhub-ensemble achieves a relative WIS of 0.61 -- 39% less probabilistic error than the COVIDhub-baseline naive model -- while no individual model consistently outperforms the ensemble across location-target-week observations. Empirical 95% prediction interval coverage for the ensemble should be 0.90.

Secondary analyses: standardized-rank density distributions across models (Fig 2), pandemic-phase stratification across the four named phases, and per-location relative-WIS heatmaps (Fig 5).

No models are being fit here -- the forecasts are pre-computed model outputs from teams. This is purely a scoring and aggregation task, with R 4.0.2 (the analysis software stack used by Reich Lab) as the reference reproducibility environment. Compute requirements are minimal -- single workstation, no GPU.

Claims: probabilistic forecast skill comparison only. No public-health policy recommendations.
@graph.name:
computational biology — EHR clinical-prediction analysis: derive labels from raw EHR tables
(e.g., Sepsis-3 onset via the mimic-code derived table), extract
windowed vital-sign / lab features with a mandatory leakage audit,
fit one or more model families (gradient-boosted trees plus a
sequence model), then audit calibration, fairness, and external
generalization on a separate holdout cohort. The archetype models
the canonical YAIB-style EHR-prediction pipeline (van de Water 2024
ICLR); concrete model and label choices stay method-neutral.

Agent Instructions
Workflow Context
Discovery and scoring policies
Execution provenance log
Discover method for predictive_model_fit (options: xgboost, lightgbm, lstm, tcn, transformer). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for fairness_audit (options: fairlearn, aequitas, custom_subgroup_bootstrap). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for external_validation (options: sklearn_metrics, custom_external_scorer). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for clinical_label_derivation (options: mimic_code_sepsis3, eicu_helpers, custom_sql_concepts). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for clinical_feature_extraction (options: mimic_extract, concepts_library, fiddle, custom_window_aggregator). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for calibration_audit (options: sklearn_calibration_curve, netcal, custom_platt, custom_isotonic). Surface set_intake_method.

Import structured tabular or domain-formatted inputs (CDISC ADaM/SDTM, time-series
panels, pre-computed count matrices) supplied by the SME. Mirrors `data_acquisition`
but for the clinical-trial and time-series-forecast project classes where inputs
are files-on-disk rather than repository accessions.

Validate outputs of: Import structured tabular or domain-formatted inputs (CDISC ADaM/SDTM, time-series
panels, pre-computed count matrices) supplied by the SME. Mirrors `data_acquisition`
but for the clinical-trial and time-series-forecast project classes where inputs
are files-on-disk rather than repository accessions.

Derive ground-truth labels (e.g., Sepsis-3 onset, hospital mortality,
AKI stage) from raw EHR tables. Canonical derivations come from
community-maintained code (e.g., mimic-code `sepsis3` table, eICU
helpers); ICD-only labels are a known failure mode and must be
rejected by the agent's runtime check. Emits a labeled cohort
table with one row per (patient, encounter, prediction window)
and the derived outcome columns + onset timestamps.

Validate outputs of: Derive ground-truth labels (e.g., Sepsis-3 onset, hospital mortality,
AKI stage) from raw EHR tables. Canonical derivations come from
community-maintained code (e.g., mimic-code `sepsis3` table, eICU
helpers); ICD-only labels are a known failure mode and must be
rejected by the agent's runtime check. Emits a labeled cohort
table with one row per (patient, encounter, prediction window)
and the derived outcome columns + onset timestamps.

Windowed vital-sign / lab / treatment feature extraction for clinical
prediction. Builds a per-(patient, prediction-window) feature matrix
from labeled-cohort timestamps, optionally aggregated through standard
EHR feature frameworks (MIMIC-Extract, concepts library, FIDDLE).
Runs a mandatory leakage audit that asserts no feature window overlaps
the label-derivation window (e.g., the Sepsis-3 SOFA + suspected
infection window); the audit emits a per-feature `crosses_label_window`
flag and refuses to emit features that cross the boundary.

Validate outputs of: Windowed vital-sign / lab / treatment feature extraction for clinical
prediction. Builds a per-(patient, prediction-window) feature matrix
from labeled-cohort timestamps, optionally aggregated through standard
EHR feature frameworks (MIMIC-Extract, concepts library, FIDDLE).
Runs a mandatory leakage audit that asserts no feature window overlaps
the label-derivation window (e.g., the Sepsis-3 SOFA + suspected
infection window); the audit emits a per-feature `crosses_label_window`
flag and refuses to emit features that cross the boundary.

Fit one or more clinical-prediction model families against the
windowed feature matrix. Method-neutral: the agent picks the model
family from the SME-named candidate list (typically gradient-boosted
trees — XGBoost / LightGBM — plus a sequence model — LSTM / TCN /
Transformer) at runtime. Emits trained-model artifacts and per-row
predicted probabilities on an internal validation split; cross-
validation and hyperparameter-search logs are committed to the
fitting report.

Validate outputs of: Fit one or more clinical-prediction model families against the
windowed feature matrix. Method-neutral: the agent picks the model
family from the SME-named candidate list (typically gradient-boosted
trees — XGBoost / LightGBM — plus a sequence model — LSTM / TCN /
Transformer) at runtime. Emits trained-model artifacts and per-row
predicted probabilities on an internal validation split; cross-
validation and hyperparameter-search logs are committed to the
fitting report.

Audit subgroup performance differentials for a fitted clinical
prediction model. Computes AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity, specificity,
and calibration metrics per stratification axis (typically age,
sex, race/ethnicity, hospital-of-origin) plus pairwise subgroup
differentials with bootstrap confidence intervals. Emits a
fairness report and a SME-facing pass/fail per configured
differential thresholds. Subgroup membership comes from the
labeled-cohort sensitive-attribute columns; absent stratifiers
surface as a configurable blocker rather than silently dropping
the axis.

Re-evaluate a trained predictive model on a holdout external cohort
(e.g., eICU-CRD when the model was trained on MIMIC-IV). Repeats
label derivation, feature extraction, and prediction generation
using the SAME pipeline contracts as the internal pass, then scores
the model's predictions against the external cohort's labels.
Emits AUROC, AUPRC, calibration, fairness metrics plus a transport
diagnostic (feature-distribution shift, label-prevalence shift)
so SMEs see whether degradation is statistical drop or distribution
shift. External AUROC is expected to drop 0.05-0.15 relative to
internal validation; this is not interpreted as model failure
absent other signals.

Audit the calibration of a fitted predictive model on the internal
validation predictions. Emits the Brier score, the reliability curve
(binned predicted vs observed), expected and maximum calibration
error (ECE / MCE), and a SME-facing pass/fail per a configured
Brier threshold (e.g., Brier ≤ 0.10 for binary clinical prediction).
Recommends a calibration method (Platt / isotonic / temperature
scaling) only when the audit fails the threshold; recommendation
is advisory — calibration retraining is gated by SME approval.

Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Validate outputs of: Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.

Validate outputs of: Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.
